---
title: "MP003: Operating Modes"
subtitle: "System execution contexts with different access controls and behaviors"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "MP"
number: "003"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-16"
author: "Claude"
type: "meta-principle"
derives_from:
  - "MP000: Axiomatization System"
  - "MP001: Primitive Terms and Definitions"
influences:
  - "MP004: Mode Hierarchy"
  - "MP006: Data Source Hierarchy"
  - "P008: Deployment Patterns"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Core Concept {#core-concept}

The precision marketing system operates under different modes that control access to data sources, feature availability, and operational behavior. These modes create clear boundaries for different types of operations and enhance security by restricting access based on operational context.

:::{.callout-important}
## Three Operating Modes
The system defines three distinct operating modes, each with specific purposes and access controls.
:::

# Mode Definitions {#mode-definitions}

## APP_MODE (Application Mode) {#app-mode}

:::{.callout-note icon="false"}
## 🎯 Purpose
Run Shiny applications in production-like environments

### Features
- **Read-only access** to app_data and Data.duckdb
- **No access** to raw_data, cleansed_data, or processed_data
- Loads **limited subset** of global_scripts
- Minimal dependencies
- Security-first approach
:::

## UPDATE_MODE (Update Mode) {#update-mode}

:::{.callout-note icon="false"}
## 🔄 Purpose
Process data, update application data sources, and develop application components

### Features
- **Read-write access** to app_data and Data.duckdb
- **Read-write access** to raw_data, cleansed_data, and processed_data
- **Full access** to directories 01_db through 14_sql_utils
- Loads broader set of utilities
- Focused on data processing capabilities
:::

## GLOBAL_MODE (Global Mode) {#global-mode}

:::{.callout-note icon="false"}
## 🌐 Purpose
Manage global resources and cross-project utilities

### Features
- **Full access** to all data sources
- Administrative capabilities for global resources
- Access to 30_global_data directory for cross-project data
- Loads extended development utilities
- Used for system maintenance
:::

# Mode Selection {#mode-selection}

The operating mode is controlled by the `OPERATION_MODE` environment variable and should be set at the beginning of execution:

```r
# Explicitly set operation mode
OPERATION_MODE <- "APP_MODE"  # or "UPDATE_MODE" or "GLOBAL_MODE"

# Load appropriate initialization script
source(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "00_principles", 
                 "sc_initialization_app_mode.R"))
```

:::{.callout-tip}
## Initialization Script Mapping
Each mode has a corresponding initialization script:

1. `sc_initialization_app_mode.R` → APP_MODE
2. `sc_initialization_update_mode.R` → UPDATE_MODE
3. `sc_initialization_global_mode.R` → GLOBAL_MODE
:::

# Mode-Specific Initialization {#mode-initialization}

Each initialization script loads a specific set of dependencies:

:::{.panel-tabset}

## APP_MODE Initialization

```r
# sc_initialization_app_mode.R (simplified)
OPERATION_MODE <- "APP_MODE"

# Load only what Shiny app needs
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "02_db_utils"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "04_utils"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "03_config"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "10_rshinyapp_components"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "11_rshinyapp_utils"))

# Enforce read-only access
read_only_db_access <- TRUE

# Restrict data directory access
allow_raw_data_access <- FALSE
allow_processed_data_access <- FALSE
```

## UPDATE_MODE Initialization

```r
# sc_initialization_update_mode.R (simplified)
OPERATION_MODE <- "UPDATE_MODE"

# Load all data processing utilities
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "01_db"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "02_db_utils"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "03_config"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "04_utils"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "05_import"))
# ... additional directories

# Allow data modification
read_only_db_access <- FALSE

# Allow access to processing data
allow_raw_data_access <- TRUE
allow_processed_data_access <- TRUE
```

## GLOBAL_MODE Initialization

```r
# sc_initialization_global_mode.R (simplified)
OPERATION_MODE <- "GLOBAL_MODE"

# Load everything including global resource utilities
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "01_db"))
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "02_db_utils"))
# ... all directories
source_directory(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "99_development"))

# Full access
read_only_db_access <- FALSE
allow_raw_data_access <- TRUE
allow_processed_data_access <- TRUE
allow_global_data_modification <- TRUE
```

:::

# Mode-Based Security Controls {#security-controls}

Operating modes enforce security controls:

## Database Access Controls {#database-controls}

```r
# Example from dbConnect_from_list.R
dbConnect_from_list <- function(db_name, read_only = NULL) {
  # Default to mode-specific setting if not specified
  if (is.null(read_only)) {
    read_only <- read_only_db_access
  }
  
  # APP_MODE forces app_data to be read-only
  if (OPERATION_MODE == "APP_MODE" && db_name == "app_data") {
    read_only <- TRUE
  }
  
  # Prevent raw data access in APP_MODE
  if (OPERATION_MODE == "APP_MODE" && 
      db_name %in% c("raw_data", "cleansed_data", "processed_data")) {
    stop("Access to ", db_name, " is not allowed in APP_MODE")
  }
  
  # Connect with appropriate permissions
  duckdb::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path, read_only = read_only)
}
```

## File System Controls {#filesystem-controls}

```r
# Example file access check
check_file_access <- function(file_path) {
  # Prevent raw data access in APP_MODE
  if (OPERATION_MODE == "APP_MODE" && 
      grepl("raw_data|cleansed_data|processed_data", file_path)) {
    stop("Access to ", file_path, " is not allowed in APP_MODE")
  }
  
  # Restrict global data modification
  if (OPERATION_MODE != "GLOBAL_MODE" && 
      grepl("30_global_data", file_path) && 
      file.access(file_path, 2) == 0) {  # Check write permission
    stop("Modification of global data is only allowed in GLOBAL_MODE")
  }
  
  # Allow access if checks pass
  return(TRUE)
}
```

# Best Practices {#best-practices}

## Explicit Mode Declaration {#explicit-declaration}

Always explicitly set and verify the operation mode:

```r
# At the beginning of each script
OPERATION_MODE <- "UPDATE_MODE"  # Choose appropriate mode

# Verify initialization is complete
if (!exists("INITIALIZATION_COMPLETED") || !INITIALIZATION_COMPLETED) {
  source(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "00_principles", 
                   paste0("sc_initialization_", tolower(OPERATION_MODE), ".R")))
}
```

## Mode-Appropriate Operations {#mode-appropriate}

Respect mode boundaries and only perform operations suitable for the current mode:

```r
# Good practice - check mode before operations
if (OPERATION_MODE == "APP_MODE") {
  # Read-only operations
  display_data(read_app_data())
} else if (OPERATION_MODE %in% c("UPDATE_MODE", "GLOBAL_MODE")) {
  # Data modification operations
  update_app_data()
}
```

## Mode Testing {#mode-testing}

Test components in all relevant modes:

```r
# Function to test across modes
test_component_in_all_modes <- function(component_name) {
  # Save current mode
  original_mode <- OPERATION_MODE
  
  # Test in APP_MODE
  OPERATION_MODE <- "APP_MODE"
  source(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "00_principles", 
                   "sc_initialization_app_mode.R"))
  test_result_app <- test_component(component_name)
  
  # Test in UPDATE_MODE
  OPERATION_MODE <- "UPDATE_MODE"
  source(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "00_principles", 
                   "sc_initialization_update_mode.R"))
  test_result_update <- test_component(component_name)
  
  # Restore original mode
  OPERATION_MODE <- original_mode
  source(file.path("update_scripts", "global_scripts", "00_principles", 
                   paste0("sc_initialization_", tolower(OPERATION_MODE), ".R")))
  
  # Return test results
  list(app_mode = test_result_app, update_mode = test_result_update)
}
```

# Mode Comparison Table {#mode-comparison}

:::{.callout-tip}
## Quick Reference
:::

| Feature | APP_MODE | UPDATE_MODE | GLOBAL_MODE |
|---------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Run applications | Process data | System maintenance |
| **app_data Access** | Read-only | Read-write | Read-write |
| **raw_data Access** | ❌ None | ✅ Read-write | ✅ Read-write |
| **processed_data Access** | ❌ None | ✅ Read-write | ✅ Read-write |
| **30_global_data Access** | ❌ None | Read-only | ✅ Read-write |
| **Scripts Loaded** | Minimal set | Data processing set | Complete set |
| **Security Level** | Highest | Medium | Lowest |
| **Typical Users** | End users | Data analysts | System administrators |

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Operating Modes principle establishes clear separation between different execution contexts, enhancing security, maintainability, and functionality of the precision marketing system. By respecting mode boundaries, we ensure applications run with appropriate access controls and dependencies optimized for their specific purpose.

:::{.callout-warning}
## Important Reminder
Always select the appropriate operating mode based on task requirements and ensure code respects mode boundaries to maintain system security and integrity.
:::
